questions.q: CVE-2016-1287 affected Cisco ASA's handling of which protocol?
options: IKE (Internet Key Exchange) for VPN tunnel setup
SNMP for device management polling

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2016-1287 required valid authentication credentials before the heap overflow could be triggered.
options: False — the attack required no authentication whatsoever
True — an IKE session had to be established first

questions.q: Which ports did CVE-2016-1287 expose as attack surface on Cisco ASA?
options: UDP/500 (IKE) and UDP/4500 (NAT-T)
TCP/443 (HTTPS) and TCP/22 (SSH)

questions.q: The Shadow Brokers' EXTRABACON tool exploited CVE-2016-1287. What group is believed to have originally developed it?
options: The NSA (National Security Agency)
The CIA's Vault 7 division

questions.q: True or False: On 64-bit Cisco ASA platforms, CVE-2016-1287 typically resulted in remote code execution rather than a denial of service.
options: False — 64-bit platforms typically experienced DoS (device reload), not RCE
True — 64-bit platforms were more exploitable than 32-bit ones

questions.q: CVE-2017-3881 exploited which Cisco protocol's Telnet option parser?
options: CMP — Cluster Management Protocol
CDP — Cisco Discovery Protocol

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2017-3881 was only exploitable if the administrator had explicitly enabled Telnet on the device.
options: False — the vulnerable CMP parsing ran even when Telnet was disabled via config
True — disabling Telnet with 'no service telnet' fully mitigated the vulnerability

questions.q: How was CVE-2017-3881 brought to public attention?
options: WikiLeaks published Vault 7, which referenced a Cisco CMP exploit
The Shadow Brokers leaked the exploit alongside EternalBlue

questions.q: Approximately how long had the CVE-2017-3881 vulnerability existed in Cisco IOS before it was patched?
options: Over 20 years — introduced around 1996
About 3 years — introduced around 2014

questions.q: True or False: The correct mitigation for CVE-2017-3881 was to use 'no service telnet' in the IOS config.
options: False — a vty ACL blocking TCP/23 was required because the vulnerable code still ran regardless
True — 'no service telnet' fully stopped CMP option processing

questions.q: CDPwn CVE-2020-3118 was a format string vulnerability triggered by what kind of data in a CDP packet?
options: A malicious format specifier (e.g. %n) in the Device-ID TLV field
An oversized Platform TLV field that overflowed a stack buffer

questions.q: True or False: CDP operates at Layer 3, so a properly configured firewall can block CDP-based attacks.
options: False — CDP is a Layer 2 protocol; firewalls and ACLs provide no protection against it
True — an ACL on the router interface will drop CDP frames

questions.q: Which Cisco operating system was specifically targeted by CVE-2020-3118, commonly found on carrier-grade routers?
options: Cisco IOS XR — used on ASR 9000 and NCS 5000 series
Cisco NX-OS — used on Nexus datacenter switches

questions.q: What security firm disclosed the CDPwn vulnerability suite in February 2020?
options: Armis Security
Sygnia Incident Response

questions.q: True or False: The format string specifier %n is dangerous because it reads memory from the stack.
options: False — %n writes the count of bytes printed to an address, enabling arbitrary memory writes
True — %n is a read-only format specifier used for memory inspection

questions.q: CVE-2021-1609 affected the web management interface of which Cisco product line?
options: Cisco RV340/RV345 — small and medium business VPN routers
Cisco ASA 5500-X — enterprise perimeter firewalls

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2021-1609 required a valid administrator password to reach the vulnerable diagnostic API endpoint.
options: False — the vulnerable /api/v1/diag_* endpoints processed requests before authentication was checked
True — the attacker needed at least a read-only account to reach the endpoint

questions.q: What underlying OS did the Cisco RV340/RV345 run, making OS command injection directly impactful?
options: Linux (BusyBox) — code execution ran as root with no privilege separation
VxWorks RTOS — a proprietary real-time OS with limited shell access

questions.q: Which threat actors were documented using CVE-2021-1609 as an initial access vector?
options: Ransomware groups — using compromised SOHO routers as persistent entry points
Nation-state APT actors — targeting government branch offices exclusively

questions.q: True or False: Disabling remote management on SOHO routers eliminates the attack surface for CVE-2021-1609.
options: True — no workaround existed other than disabling remote management or applying the firmware patch
False — the vulnerability was also exploitable from the LAN side of the router

questions.q: CVE-2020-3259 was a memory disclosure flaw in Cisco ASA/FTD. What did the leaked heap memory commonly contain?
options: Active VPN session tokens and cached credentials
RSA private keys used for certificate-based VPN authentication

questions.q: True or False: The Akira ransomware group began exploiting CVE-2020-3259 within weeks of its original May 2020 disclosure.
options: False — Akira exploited it approximately four years after disclosure in 2024
True — CVE-2020-3259 was actively exploited the same year it was patched

questions.q: What attack technique did CVE-2020-3259 enable that specifically bypassed multi-factor authentication?
options: Session token theft — a stolen active session cookie grants access regardless of how it was obtained
Credential brute-force — leaked passwords were used to replay authentication

questions.q: Which interface on the Cisco ASA was targeted by CVE-2020-3259?
options: The WebVPN/AnyConnect web services interface (HTTPS)
The ASDM management interface on a dedicated management port

questions.q: True or False: Once a valid VPN session token is obtained via memory disclosure, the attacker still needs to complete MFA to establish the tunnel.
options: False — a valid session token grants authenticated access; MFA was already completed when the token was issued
True — Cisco ASA validates MFA on every connection regardless of session token state

questions.q: CVE-2019-1663 exploited a stack overflow in which component of the Cisco RV110W/RV130W?
options: The web-based management interface HTTP request handler
The IPsec VPN key negotiation module

questions.q: True or False: The Cisco RV110W and RV130W routers included stack canaries and ASLR, making CVE-2019-1663 difficult to exploit reliably.
options: False — these MIPS-based devices lacked canaries, ASLR, and NX/DEP, making exploitation trivial
True — modern exploit mitigations were present but bypassed using a ROP chain

questions.q: What was the size of the vulnerable stack buffer in the RV130W HTTP handler, and how many bytes were needed to overflow it?
options: 256-byte buffer — sending 500+ bytes in the POST body triggered the overflow
512-byte buffer — a 1024-byte payload was required to overwrite the return address

questions.q: What privilege level did a successful CVE-2019-1663 exploit achieve on the router?
options: Root (uid=0) — SOHO routers run all services as root with no privilege separation
A limited web service account — further privilege escalation was required

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2019-1663 required network access to the LAN interface; the vulnerability was not exploitable from the WAN.
options: False — attackers with access to the WAN-facing management interface could exploit the vulnerability remotely
True — the HTTP handler only bound to the LAN-side management interface

questions.q: CVE-2022-20812 was a path traversal vulnerability in which Cisco product's cluster database API?
options: Cisco Expressway Series / TelePresence VCS — enterprise video conferencing infrastructure
Cisco Unified Communications Manager (CUCM) — call processing software

questions.q: True or False: Path traversal attacks work by using '../' sequences to navigate outside the intended directory boundary.
options: True — directory traversal sequences like ../../../../etc/shadow escape the allowed file root
False — path traversal exploits symbolic links rather than directory navigation sequences

questions.q: Beyond reading sensitive files like /etc/shadow, what additional capability did CVE-2022-20812's file write path enable?
options: Writing cron jobs or init scripts for persistent root access across reboots
Overwriting firmware images to install a backdoor in the boot loader

questions.q: Where is Cisco Expressway typically deployed in a network architecture?
options: In the DMZ — it handles firewall traversal and SIP/H.323 routing for video conferencing
On the internal LAN — it only processes traffic from trusted internal hosts

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2022-20812 could be exploited by a completely unauthenticated attacker with no credentials.
options: False — the attack required cluster API credentials with read-write privileges
True — the Expressway API was reachable without any authentication

questions.q: CVE-2024-20399 exploited unsanitized parameters in which Cisco operating system's CLI?
options: Cisco NX-OS — the OS running on Nexus datacenter switches
Cisco IOS XE — the OS running on enterprise edge routers

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2024-20399 allowed unauthenticated attackers to gain root access on Cisco Nexus switches.
options: False — authenticated access with privilege level 5 or higher was required to reach the vulnerable command
True — the injection could be triggered without any credentials via the REST API

questions.q: What made CVE-2024-20399 particularly dangerous despite the authentication requirement?
options: It broke out of NX-OS RBAC entirely, granting Linux root access invisible to NX-OS monitoring
It allowed privilege escalation from any NX-OS account to administrator within the NX-OS shell

questions.q: The Velvet Ant APT maintained persistence in a Fortune 500 company's data center switches for how long before detection?
options: Over 3 years — since approximately 2021, discovered in 2024
About 6 months — a routine security audit caught unusual NX-OS process behavior

questions.q: True or False: Persistence written to /etc/rc.d/rc.local on NX-OS survives device reboots and NX-OS software upgrades.
options: True — the underlying Linux OS init scripts persist independently of the NX-OS software layer
False — NX-OS upgrades overwrite the entire Linux filesystem including rc.local

questions.q: In the ArcaneDoor campaign, CVE-2024-20353 was used to crash Cisco ASA firewalls. What was the primary operational goal of this crash?
options: Clear in-memory logs to create a detection blind spot before follow-on operations
Permanently disable the firewall to allow unrestricted network access

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2024-20353 required valid admin credentials to send the malformed HTTP request that crashed the ASA.
options: False — the malformed multipart HTTP request could be sent by an unauthenticated attacker
True — only authenticated management sessions could reach the vulnerable HTTP endpoint

questions.q: What combination of CVEs did the ArcaneDoor attackers use to achieve persistent access after the firewall crash?
options: CVE-2024-20353 (DoS/log wipe) chained with CVE-2024-20359 (persistent backdoor installation)
CVE-2024-20353 (DoS) chained with CVE-2020-3259 (memory disclosure for credential theft)

questions.q: Which threat actor was attributed to the ArcaneDoor espionage campaign targeting government firewalls?
options: UAT4356 — a Chinese state-sponsored APT (also tracked as Storm-1849)
Velvet Ant — the same group responsible for Nexus switch persistence

questions.q: True or False: Sending ASA syslogs to an external SIEM prevents log loss when the device crashes due to CVE-2024-20353.
options: True — externally forwarded syslogs survive the device reload; only the in-memory buffer is lost
False — the ASA stops all syslog transmission during the crash, so external SIEMs also miss the events

questions.q: The Line Dancer implant (CVE-2024-20359) survived Cisco ASA reboots by abusing which legacy feature?
options: A VPN client/plug-in pre-loading mechanism that executed files from flash on boot
A persistent debug mode that preserved shellcode in non-volatile NVRAM across reboots

questions.q: True or False: Line Dancer operated by writing a persistent service to the ASA configuration that appeared in 'show run' output.
options: False — Line Dancer operated entirely in memory and was invisible to standard 'show' commands
True — Line Dancer added an entry to the ASA startup configuration for persistence

questions.q: How did Line Dancer authenticate its operator to prevent accidental triggering or discovery by defenders?
options: A 'host knock' mechanism — only HTTPS POST requests with a specific Host header value triggered shellcode
A shared secret in a custom TLS certificate extension that Line Dancer verified before executing payloads

questions.q: What monitoring capabilities did Line Dancer disable on the compromised ASA to blind defenders?
options: Syslog forwarding and SNMP traps — both were disabled by the implant during operations
NetFlow exports and ASDM session logging — the implant targeted traffic visibility only

questions.q: True or False: Standard Cisco ASA 'show' commands are sufficient to detect the Line Dancer implant on a compromised device.
options: False — detection required comparing flash filesystem file hashes against Cisco-signed baselines
True — 'show version' and 'show running-config' reveal any unauthorized modules loaded by the implant

questions.q: CVE-2021-1291 is a SQL injection vulnerability in which Cisco SD-WAN component?
options: Cisco vManage — the centralized SD-WAN management and orchestration controller
Cisco vEdge — the WAN edge router that terminates SD-WAN tunnels

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2021-1291 required an attacker to have administrator-level vManage credentials to extract router PSKs.
options: False — a low-privilege read-only account was sufficient to exploit the SQL injection
True — only accounts with 'Network Admin' role could access the vulnerable API endpoint

questions.q: What type of SQL injection technique was used in CVE-2021-1291 to exfiltrate data from additional tables?
options: UNION-based injection — appending a second SELECT to the original query to retrieve out-of-scope data
Blind boolean-based injection — inferring data one bit at a time from true/false response differences

questions.q: What is the primary reason compromising vManage is catastrophic for SD-WAN environments?
options: vManage is the single pane of glass — controlling it means controlling every WAN router, policy, and tunnel in the enterprise
vManage stores only metadata; actual router credentials are distributed and protected separately

questions.q: True or False: After a confirmed vManage SQL injection compromise, it is sufficient to patch vManage without rotating WAN router PSKs.
options: False — all WAN router PSKs and tunnel credentials should be rotated since all were potentially exposed
True — patching the injection flaw prevents any previously leaked PSKs from being used by attackers

questions.q: CVE-2023-20109 exploited the GDOI protocol used by which Cisco VPN feature?
options: GET VPN (Group Encrypted Transport VPN) — which uses group-shared keys across all member routers
FlexVPN — which uses IKEv2 for point-to-point hub-and-spoke tunnels

questions.q: True or False: In a GET VPN architecture, compromising the key server with CVE-2023-20109 could compromise all group member routers simultaneously.
options: True — the key server broadcasts GDOI rekey messages to all members; a malicious rekey exploits all recipients at once
False — each group member would need to be exploited individually after the key server was compromised

questions.q: Which attribute in the GDOI rekey message contained the vulnerable field that triggered the buffer overflow in CVE-2023-20109?
options: The KEK (Key Encryption Key) attribute — which was not properly validated by group member routers
The TEK (Traffic Encryption Key) attribute — which carried the actual session encryption material

questions.q: What delivery mechanism did CVE-2023-20109 use to reach all group members simultaneously?
options: IP multicast — GDOI rekey messages are sent to a multicast group address like 239.0.0.1
Unicast TCP — the key server established individual TCP sessions to each group member for rekey delivery

questions.q: True or False: Segmenting GET VPN groups by criticality is a recommended practice to limit the blast radius of a key server compromise.
options: True — smaller groups mean fewer routers are compromised if a malicious rekey is broadcast
False — GET VPN requires all routers to share the same group key, so segmentation is not architecturally possible

questions.q: CVE-2018-0296 was a path traversal vulnerability in which component of the Cisco ASA?
options: The ASA web interface — accessible via /+CSCOE+/ and /+CSCOT+/ URL paths
The ASDM Java management client — triggered by a specially crafted ASDM launch URL

questions.q: True or False: CVE-2018-0296 could only trigger a denial of service and did not expose any information about the ASA.
options: False — it also enabled directory listing of the ASA filesystem, revealing installed modules and VPN plugins
True — the path traversal only caused a device reload with no information disclosure capability

questions.q: What URL encoding technique did CVE-2018-0296 rely on to bypass naive input filters?
options: Percent-encoding — using %2F for '/' and %2E%2E for '..' to evade filters checking for literal characters
Unicode overlong encoding — using multi-byte UTF-8 sequences to represent traversal characters

questions.q: How long did it typically take for automated scanners to begin probing for CVE-2018-0296 after public disclosure?
options: Within 48 hours — mass scanning began almost immediately after the June 2018 advisory
Several weeks — time was needed for exploit developers to reverse-engineer and weaponize the vulnerability

questions.q: True or False: A Cisco ASA firewall reload caused by CVE-2018-0296 results in a brief period with no ACL enforcement or VPN inspection.
options: True — during the approximately 3-minute reload, all stateful inspection, ACLs, and VPN tunnels are suspended
False — Cisco ASA uses a warm standby process that maintains enforcement continuity during software reloads
